_comment: Registry for the multi-report build (Phase 2). NOT yet read by build.py — the current pipeline still runs from sectors.json + master_prompt.md and is untouched. Each report = its own prompt + its own page at site/<slug>/. 'description' is the plain-English blurb shown on the hub card. 'run_time_et' is the canonical schedule (ET); 'run_time_cr' is the Costa Rica equivalent used to set cron-job.org. 'enabled' gates what the new build runs; 'status' tracks readiness.

reports.name: Sector Intelligence Report
reports.description: A single, at-a-glance read on where money is flowing across the entire US stock market. It scores all 11 sectors (plus hot themes like AI, semiconductors, and crypto) from strongly bullish to strongly bearish, ranks them against each other, and drills into the leaders and laggards with the specific stocks most likely to move — each with a conviction score, the catalyst, key price levels, and the next earnings date. Think of it as a daily market leaderboard: who's leading, who's lagging, and where the best swing-trade setups are (3-day to 6-week horizon). Educational only — not investment advice.
reports.status: ACTIVE — this consolidated report is now the daily sector build: build.yml runs `python build.py --report sector-intelligence` (cron-job.org primary; GitHub backup cron currently disabled). Replaces the old 5 separate sector pages (legacy sectors.json build retained for manual use but no longer scheduled).

reports.name: Gap Risk Report
reports.description: This report tells you how big a jump — up or down — the four major US stock indexes (the S&P 500, Nasdaq 100, Dow, and Russell 2000) might make overnight, before the market opens tomorrow. For each one it gives you a simple risk dial (Calm to Extreme), the likely size of the move, which way it's leaning, and the key price levels to watch. Think of it as a weather forecast for tomorrow morning's open: it shows you which storms are brewing and where, in plain English — without claiming to know exactly what will happen.
reports.status: ACTIVE — scheduled via report.yml ({"reports":"gap-risk"}) at 11:30am ET. Live and tested end-to-end.

reports.name: Mean Reversion Report
reports.description: The Mean-Reversion Scan hunts the market's most stretched optionable assets — stocks, ETFs, commodities, rates, currencies, volatility, and crypto — and flags the ones that have run too far, too fast and are likely to snap back toward their average. It screens for overbought and oversold extremes, filters out the "falling knives" that look cheap but keep falling, factors in the prevailing macro backdrop, and surfaces a ranked watchlist of candidates worth a closer look — all for educational purposes, not investment advice.
reports.status: ACTIVE — scheduled via report.yml in the 8:40am ET morning batch ({"reports":"sector-intelligence,mean-reversion"}). Live and tested end-to-end.

reports.name: Pre-Market Report
reports.prompt: mwtc/ (bot — not a prompts/ pipeline report)
reports.description: The MWTC Daily AI Report — pre-market edition. An institutional-style options-trading briefing in the Michael Wade voice. It pulls live market data (futures, macro, technicals, Unusual Whales options/whale flow, earnings, Fed, and sentiment) and lays out the day ahead: index bias dials, key levels, unusual-options activity, and defined-risk trade ideas. Every figure is data-bound and verified — no fabricated numbers. Educational only, not investment advice.
reports.status: produced by the MWTC bot (mwtc/) via .github/workflows/mwtc.yml --mode premarket, published to site/pre-market/. Needs UW_API_KEY (+ optional FINNHUB/NASDAQ/FMP) secrets for the institutional layer; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY already set. Pending: set the UW key + add the cron-job.org trigger (9:00am ET).

reports.name: Market Wrap Report
reports.prompt: mwtc/ (bot — not a prompts/ pipeline report)
reports.description: The MWTC Daily AI Report — post-market edition. The same institutional options briefing in the Michael Wade voice, run after the close: how the market finished, after-hours earnings reactions, a sector scorecard, unusual-options activity, and the setup into the next session. Every figure is data-bound and verified — no fabricated numbers. Educational only, not investment advice.
reports.status: produced by the MWTC bot (mwtc/) via .github/workflows/mwtc.yml --mode postmarket, published to site/market-wrap/. Needs UW_API_KEY (+ optional FINNHUB/NASDAQ/FMP) secrets; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY already set. Pending: set the UW key + add the cron-job.org trigger (4:30pm ET).
